title: Is a Beat in Store for Molson Coors (TAP) in Q2 Earnings?
text: Despite inflation, Molson Coors' (TAP) second-quarter results are expected to reflect strength in core brands, a revitalization plan and the premiumization of its global portfolio.

title: Target: Optimistic Consumers Pulling Back, Retail Earnings On Tap, Shares A Hold
text: The July Consumer Confidence report showed high optimism, but retail sales figures show a negative annual change, with a decrease of 0.40% in the week ending July 22, 2023. Target faces potential issues from food deflation and product theft, but its valuation case is compelling, with better than expected bottom-line numbers and a high forward dividend yield. Despite the stock being undervalued, poor retail trends and a bearish chart lead to a hold rating on Target, with a potential revisit post-earnings later in the year.

title: Molson Coors Appoints Natalie Maciolek Chief Legal and Government Affairs Officer
text: CHICAGO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Molson Coors today announced Natalie Maciolek will join the company's global leadership team as Chief Legal and Government Affairs officer, effective September 5. Maciolek, who most recently served as senior vice president, general counsel and corporate secretary of Wisconsin-based Kohler Co., will report to Molson Coors President and Chief Executive Officer, Gavin Hattersley, and will be based in Chicago. As the company's top attorney, Maciolek will oversee all legal.

title: Molson Coors: Cheers After A Hangover At Its Competitor
text: Molson Coors Beverage Company has seen a modest recovery coming out of the pandemic. The company has been struggling along until its major beer competitor Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV made a major marketing mistake in recent weeks. This short-term windfall should become apparent in the coming quarterly results, but given the underwhelming operational performance, current enthusiasm seems to provide a nice exit opportunity.

title: Molson Coors Beverage Company Announces Regular Quarterly Dividend

title: 5 Stocks to Watch as the Alcohol Industry Trends Turn Unfavorable
